user_message:
You are running as part of compound-engineering:ce-doc-review. Review the document `docs/plans/2026-06-20-002-docs-comprehensive-project-manual-plan.md`.

Document type: plan
Origin: none
Decision primer: <prior-decisions>Round 1 — no prior decisions.</prior-decisions>

Return ONLY valid JSON with keys: reviewer, findings, residual_risks, deferred_questions. Findings must use severity P0/P1/P2/P3, finding_type error/omission, autofix_class safe_auto/gated_auto/manual, confidence 50/75/100 only, and evidence as an array of direct quotes from the document. Suppress style-only or speculative findings. Focus on falsifying load-bearing premises, unstated assumptions, decision stress tests, and alternative blindness. Do not duplicate security, feasibility, or scope findings unless the epistemic gap is distinct.
assistant_summary:
{
  "reviewer": "adversarial",
  "findings": [
    {
      "title": "Evidence granularity can fail silently",
      "severity": "P1",
      "section": "Requirements / W1 / Success Metrics",
      "why_it_matters": "Reviewers can sign off on a chapter-level source matrix while individual current-behavior claims remain unbacked, because the requirement says every claim needs evidence but the measurable checks only require chapter sources. That lets W1/M3 pass while R13/M5 fails silently, which is the drift failure the plan is trying to avoid.",
      "finding_type": "error",
      "autofix_class": "gated_auto",
      "suggested_fix": "Revise W1, R15, and M3 so the evidence ledger has claim-level rows for each current-behavior or drift-sensitive claim, with chapter-level source lists treated only as navigation.",
      "confidence": 75,
      "evidence": [
        "R13. Every current-behavior claim is backed by a code path, test, or current authoritative doc.",
        "Every manual chapter has named source files.",
        "M3. Each chapter has at least one named source in the evidence matrix.",
        "M5. Manual review finds no unsupported current-behavior claims."
      ]
    },
    {
      "title": "Decision sources are unverified",
      "severity": "P1",
      "section": "Source-of-Truth Map / Work Breakdown",
      "why_it_matters": "Implementers may treat opaque decision IDs as authoritative without verifying the records exist, are fresh, or say what the plan implies. Because those IDs carry the product narrative and contract-drift rationale, an unavailable or superseded decision would make the manual source-backed in form but not in fact.",
      "finding_type": "omission",
      "autofix_class": "gated_auto",
      "suggested_fix": "Add a W1 acceptance check that resolves every cited decision ID, records its title and staleness status in the evidence ledger, and removes or marks unavailable any decision that cannot be verified.",
      "confidence": 75,
      "evidence": [
        "Use these sources before writing claims. Prefer the code or contract test when a prose doc and code disagree.",
        "Product identity and wedge | `README.md`, `ROADMAP.md`, decision `629f4a79-61b5-46d5-8a22-8311bb83d1ae`",
        "Inputs: `README.md`, `ROADMAP.md`, `docs/decisions_outcomes.md`, decision `629f4a79-61b5-46d5-8a22-8311bb83d1ae`.",
        "Inputs: `src/entirecontext/core/decisions.py`, `src/entirecontext/cli/decisions_cmds.py`, `src/entirecontext/cli/futures_cmds.py`, `docs/decisions_outcomes.md`, `src/entirecontext/sync/*`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `pyproject.toml`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `docs/adr/README.md`, decision `b148ba4d-702c-4dac-97a7-aa5551a70246`, decision `dffae62d-0d8b-4c17-9102-f77eca1532e8`."
      ]
    },
    {
      "title": "Review-matrix split is unfalsifiable",
      "severity": "P2",
      "section": "Target Deliverable / Open Questions / Stop Condition",
      "why_it_matters": "Implementers must choose whether to embed or split the ledger during execution, but the plan gives only subjective labels. That creates a real disagreement point at stop condition: one pass can claim the review matrix was unnecessary while another expects it for audit speed, with no falsifying threshold.",
      "finding_type": "omission",
      "autofix_class": "manual",
      "suggested_fix": null,
      "confidence": 75,
      "evidence": [
        "Working review matrix: `docs/entirecontext-project-manual-review.md`, if the evidence ledger becomes too large for the manual appendix",
        "Default: embed a compact appendix; split to `docs/entirecontext-project-manual-review.md` only if the appendix becomes unwieldy.",
        "Stop when `docs/entirecontext-project-manual.md` exists, the optional review matrix exists only if needed"
      ]
    },
    {
      "title": "Reader promise lacks a test",
      "severity": "P2",
      "section": "Verification Plan / Success Metrics",
      "why_it_matters": "The plan can satisfy all source and coverage checks while never testing whether the intended reader can reconstruct the system from the manual alone. Since the reader promise is the problem being solved, W8 needs a task-based reader smoke check or the manual can become source-backed but still fail its stated purpose.",
      "finding_type": "omission",
      "autofix_class": "gated_auto",
      "suggested_fix": "Add a W8 manual check where a reviewer or agent uses only the draft manual to answer the M8 questions, records any missing or unsupported answers, and fixes those gaps before stop.",
      "confidence": 75,
      "evidence": [
        "Reader promise: a technically literate reader can understand what EntireContext is, why it exists, how to run it, how its memory loop works, where data lives, how agents integrate with it, and how maintainers safely change it.",
        "M8. A new reader can answer: what the tool does, how to start, where data lives, how agents use it, and how maintainers safely change it.",
        "Manual review checks",
        "Review the manual against R1-R17."
      ]
    }
  ],
  "residual_risks": [
    "The new long-form manual may still be path-dependent versus a lighter doc map or generated reference, but the document frames the manual as the requested artifact and already acknowledges stale-source risk.",
    "Use of `.agent-loop/runs` artifacts could bias research if artifacts are stale, but the plan limits them to research aids rather than authoritative sources."
  ],
  "deferred_questions": [
    "What concrete size or auditability threshold makes the separate review matrix mandatory?",
    "Who performs the M8 reader-promise smoke review, and what prior knowledge are they allowed to use?"
  ]
}
